rationale: The two ensures `forall|i|` clauses have disjoint triggers (one keyed on `post1_self_.page_array@[i]`, the other on `post2_self_.page_array@[i]`), so z3 never lines them up. Re-asserting both foralls forces trigger instantiation, then a per-index case split (i == index vs. i != index) gives pointwise equality, which `=~=` lifts to `post1_self_.page_array@ =~= post2_self_.page_array@`; every other PageAllocator field is already pinned by both runs to `pre_self_`, so the determinism predicate follows.
